steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Recall the cylinder volume formula
steps.content: The volume of a right circular cylinder is given by the product of its base area and height. From the problem: radius $r = 13\text{ m}$ and height $h = 9\text{ m}$.

steps.title: Substitute the values and calculate
steps.content:
Substitute $r = 13$ and $h = 9$ into the formula:
$$V = \pi(13)^2(9) = \pi(169)(9) = 1521\pi\text{ m}^3$$
That confirms D is correct.
